ddt:Target.Name: USA_SUV_HL_LL_v5
ddt:Target.Description:
ECU : Sonar Functions : APA, R-AEB, etc... Vehicle : [SUV] : HHN, HHC, BCB, P33A, P33B, PZ1A, DGE Supplier : Valeo SWEPL Sonar : Guillaume Mahé Authors : Guillaume Mahé Sce 65616
-V1.1 08/08/2019 initialization of the DDT data base for the Sonar [SUV] from CMFB data base For SW3
v2 07/10/2019 update for SW3.5 - most of the DIDs have been updated
v2.1 12/02/2019 - correction of DID $2060 APA - Add DID $3043 iBoosterPresence
v2.2 13/02/2019 - correction of DID $2043 size 1147bytes - DataRead.cfg.SupplierData1 variable lenght deleted in requests sheet. - DID$3051 coding updated
v2.3 04/03/2020 - correction of DID $2040 for the parameter config_calib - DID $2211 added
v3 07/04/2020 - DID $2043 size updated to 1133 bytes - DID $2044 size updated to 2045 bytes - parameter "ConfigurationFlag.RearObstacleDetection" from DID $2007 is deleted
V4 11/06/2020 - add all calibration DID linked to FAPK : $2208, $2209, $2216, $2501, $2503, $2505, $2506, $2507, $2550, $250A, $250B, $250C - add screens - add MAC Key learning routine - add cfg.SoundConfigurationFlags.DetectionBeep into DID$2006 - add DID $3200, $3201, $3202, $3001, $3002, $1000, $3003
V5 - DTC E143 HEVC deleted from Database as not used. - DTC AVM synch failure added --> A753 $81 - DID 2043, 2044 updated with final size + DID 2043 split in two (CGW buffer size limitation) - DID 2045 coding redefine with Valeo and new official size /!\ need to be careful on the database use between DC and SW - DID F194 & F195 at 32bytes to have a common Database between Sonar HL and Sonar LL - DID 2047 and 2048 have been added for the plant to be able to read DID 2043 (CGW buffer size limitation) - DID 2043 read request remain unchange. - DID 2043 write request change as follow: DID 2047 concatenate to DID 2048. change done directly in the write request. /!\maintinibility report OK for 222047 and 222048 and 2E2043 but removed for 222043 (should not be called by plant and aftersale tool) + commun Mnemonic between the DID

Item.Text: LED is light when UPA is OFF
LED is light when UPA is ON
Only once in Power ON cycle
Sleep or Crank fault, Disable DiagMuxDTC

Data.Name: RearSensorGeometry.Distance between external and central sensors
RearSensorGeometry.Distance between external and side sensors
RearSensorGeometry.Distance between side and side central sensors
FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between external and central sensors
FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between external and side sensors
FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between side and side central sensors
calib_config.Vehicle_Type
Data.Comment: define the vehicle type used in the configuration (DID$2044) or in the calibration block

Data.Name: calib_config.NumberOfSensors
Data.Comment: Define the number of sensor to be used in the calibration block

Data.Name: calib_config.calib_config
Data.Comment: Parameter to define if we use or not the calibration and so the DID $2044

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level OutOfRange/0 adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed / blocked
No possible classification about the type of fault
Open circuit or Short circuit to ground
Open circuit or Short circuit to battery

Data.Name: DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Item.Text: DriverInLoopType, DID $2060 is configured as PushButton
APA is configured for 4/6/8/10 channel system
Automatic transmission is not configured for HFPB/APK/FAPK
relation between APAVariant and APASwitchMode is not consistent
AD2, DID$3200 is configured with for 4/6/8/10 channel system

Data.Name: CalibrationNumber.Vehicle_Type
Data.Comment: define the vehicle type used in the configuration (DID$2044) or in the calibration block

Data.Name: CalibrationNumber.NumberOfSensors
Data.Comment: Define the number of sensor to be used in the calibration block

Data.Name: SupplierData_Sonar_LL_part1
Data.Comment: DID use to read the Sonar LL tuning value in plant

Data.Name: SupplierData_Sonar_LL_part2
Data.Comment: DID use to read the Sonar LL tuning value in plant

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Request.Name: DataRead.RearSensorGeometry
Request.Comment: Read RearSensorGeometryDistance between external and central sensors

DataItem.Name: RearSensorGeometry.Distance between external and central sensors
RearSensorGeometry.Distance between side and side central sensors
RearSensorGeometry.Distance between external and side sensors

Request.Name: DataRead.FrontSensorGeometry
Request.Comment: Read FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between external and central sensors

DataItem.Name: FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between external and central sensors
FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between external and side sensors
FrontSensorGeometry.Distance between side and side central sensors

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.ExtendedDiagnostic
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: RoutineControl
Request.Comment: You should remove ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.xxx data. These are examples.

Test.Description: Error key learning (Extracted from MAC specification)
Key at initial value (Extracted from MAC specification)
Shot to groud on front sensors power supply
Shot to battery on front sensors power supply
High Side Switch for Sensor Power max number of cycles reached.
Shot to groud on front sensors power supply
Shot to battery on front sensors power supply
High Side Switch for Sensor Power max number of cycles reached.
short to battery on sensor data line
short to ground on sensor data line
Over voltage at the Sensor Supply > 27V
Under voltage at the Sensor Supply < 5.2V
The Memory Blocks on the ASIC have CRC error
The Data on the Sensor is lost and reprogramming of the sensor is required
Component internal failure (Sensor ASIC / Sensor Micro)
The LIN Frames are not received on ASIC side
USV 1.0 sensor is not connected
Mismatch on sensor type detected
data line signal amplitude < minimum
CRC Error or Wrong signal value
internal sensor calibration/programming error
Magnetic nosie detected on the Sensor dure to Electromagnetic radiationsMagnetic nosie detected on the Sensor dure to Electromagnetic radiations
communication with sensor has failed
Line Frames missing
Membrane performance or incorrect operation
Ringing time of the sensor membrane after sending is too short
Ringing time of the sensor membrane after sending is too long
RTransformer on the Sensor has a failure
Sensor Blocked by Ice or Mud.
Over temperature on the Sensor detected i.e Temperature on the Sensor detected is too high
The Resonator is drifting (may be because of the drift in the Oscillator on the sensor)
short to battery on sensor data line
short to ground on sensor data line
open circuit on data line

Label.Text: Reminder : The calibration parameters are saved in RAM and so not saved after an APC OFF. At next ignition they will take back their default value
Reminder : The calibration parameters are saved in RAM and so not saved after an APC OFF. At next ignition they will take back their default value
Reminder : The calibration parameters are saved in RAM and so not saved after an APC OFF. At next ignition they will take back their default value
Reminder : The calibration parameters are saved in RAM and so not saved after an APC OFF. At next ignition they will take back their default value
